goblin.flavourLine: Marked with crude clan symbols scratched into the surface — the work of hands that have known harder times than yours.

scavenge.flavourLine: Reclaimed from somewhere it was no longer wanted. Repaired with cord and patience. It holds together well enough.

woodland.flavourLine: The grain runs deep with ironwood resin. Whatever shaped this knew the forest well.

nature.flavourLine: Something green still stirs in it. Whether that is comfort or warning depends on who is asking.

sacred.flavourLine: Someone took the time to bless this before it passed into danger. Whether it held is a matter of faith.

corrupted.flavourLine: Something in it feels slightly wrong — not broken, just displaced from what it was meant to be.

spirit.flavourLine: It carries the faint impression of something that no longer exists here. The echo is all that remains.

coastal.flavourLine: Salt has worked its way into every joint and seam. It was made for weather, and weather found it.

oath.flavourLine: It was made at the moment of a promise. Whether that promise was kept is written in the weight of it.

martial.flavourLine: Standard issue for someone, somewhere. The wear on it suggests it saw real use before ending up here.

mercenary.flavourLine: Made for someone who fights for coin, not conviction. The craftsmanship reflects exactly that priority.

dwarven.flavourLine: Dense and cold-forged at depth. Whoever made this built it to outlast the mountain, and likely will.

mountain.flavourLine: Heavy and cold-tempered at altitude. It carries the weight of the mountain it came from.

arcane.flavourLine: Faint glyphs trace the surface — not decorative. Whatever bound the spell here has mostly faded.

fire.flavourLine: The surface carries a permanent scorch pattern. It has been through fire and come out harder for it.

beast.flavourLine: Worked from hide and bone by someone who knew how to use every part of a kill. Nothing was wasted.

undead.flavourLine: Cold to the touch in a way that has nothing to do with temperature. It has been near death long enough to remember it.

orc.flavourLine: Heavy, direct, and built to last through sustained violence. The maker had no interest in decoration.

elven.flavourLine: Grown rather than made, it seems. The precision is unsettling. Nothing about it is accidental.

shadow.flavourLine: It catches light wrong. Not cursed — just shaped by hands that preferred not to be seen.
